'''David Thomas Watson''' (born 24 May 1966) is a New Zealand former [[rugby league]] footballer who represented [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the 1980s and 1990s.

==Playing career==
Watson began his career playing for the Waitara Bears in the [[Taranaki Rugby League]] before moving north and playing in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=17333 Taranaki Grand Final Countdown] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110715191357/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=17333|date=15 July 2011}} ''rleague.com'', 11 July 2003</ref> Watson joined the [[Manukau Magpies]] and in 1986 won the ARLs most improved back award.

In 1990 he signed with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for the 1990/91 season. He returned to England the next winter to play for the [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].  In 1992 he began an association with [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] that lasted three seasons.<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-teambyteam-guide-to-the-stones-bitter-championship-1543250.html Team-by-Team Guide to the Stones Bitter Championship] ''The Independent'', 29 August 1992</ref> Dave Watson played {{rlp|fb}} in [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]]'s 15-8 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992–93 League Cup (rugby league)|1992–93 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 23 January 1993.<ref name="23rd January 1993: Bradford 8 Wigan 15 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3004|title=23rd January 1993: Bradford 8 Wigan 15 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=19 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180619140552/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3004|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 1993 Watson returned to [[Taranaki rugby league team|Taranaki]] and played in the [[1993 New Zealand rugby league season|North Island Second Division]].<ref name=naki>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1993'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1993. p.p.173-174</ref> This made him eligible to participate in the Kiwi trials that year and he was selected for the series against Australia.

In 1994 Watson began playing in the Australian competition, spending time with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], [[Balmain Tigers|Sydney Tigers]], [[Gold Coast Chargers]] and [[South Queensland Crushers]].

In 1998 he returned to England, joining the [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]]. In 1998 Watson, along with fellow 1989 Kiwi [[Whetu Taewa]], Watson played {{rlp|so}} in Sheffield Eagles' 17-8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998.<ref>{{cite book
|first1=John|last1=Coffey|first2=Bernie|last2=Wood
|title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
|publisher =Huia Publishers
|year = 2008
|page = 300
|url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
|isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Watson was a [[Taranaki rugby league team|Taranaki]] and [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] representative before being selected to represent [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] in the [[1986 Pacific Cup]].<ref>{{cite book
|first1=John|last1=Coffey|first2=Bernie|last2=Wood
|title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
|publisher =Huia Publishers
|year = 2008
|page = 245
|url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
|isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}}</ref> He again represented the Māori against Great Britain in 1990.<ref>{{cite book
|first1=John|last1=Coffey|first2=Bernie|last2=Wood
|title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008
|publisher =Huia Publishers
|year = 2008
|page = 261
|url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC
|isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}}</ref>

Watson was selected for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] in 1989. He went on to make 15 appearances for the Kiwis, his last being in the 1993 test series against Australia.<ref>[http://www.nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=W&id=612 Watson, David Thomas 1989 - 91, 1993 - Kiwi #612] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724194113/http://www.nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=W&id=612 |date=24 July 2011 }} ''nzleague.co.nz''</ref>

==Legacy==
In 2008 he was named in the [[Taranaki Rugby League]] Team of the Century.<ref name="Stuff.co.nz_671468">{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/taranaki-daily-news/sport/671468/Dream-team-of-century-is-named|title=Dream team of century is named|date=14 October 2008|work= [[Taranaki Daily News]]|access-date=24 September 2011}}</ref>
